title: Urostachys inflexus
taxonomy: Urostachys inflexus is classified as a species of mat-forming, terrestrial fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It belongs to the genus Urostachys, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. Urostachys inflexus is native to tropical regions of the Americas, specifically in Central and South America. This species is often found growing in shady, humid environments. The classification of Urostachys inflexus is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its pinnate fronds and distinctive leaf venation.

intro: Urostachys inflexus, also known as the "Lycopodiaceous moss," is a type of small, terrestrial plant belonging to the club moss family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Urostachys inflexus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
